Package org.eventb.core

Interface IAccuracyElement

All Known Subinterfaces:
IPOSequent, ISCContextRoot, ISCEvent, ISCMachineRoot
All Known Implementing Classes:
POSequent, SCContextRoot, SCEvent, SCMachineRoot
public interface IAccuracyElement
An accuracy element contains a grade of accuracy: it is accurate or not. This is used by the static checker to annotate statically checked contexts (ISCContextRoot), machines (ISCMachineRoot), and events (ISCEvent) with information of whether some attribute or child element was added, removed, or modified.

The accuracy information is then propagated by the proof obligation generator to proof obligations (IPOSequent).

The accuracy is an estimation only. It may be used to inform the user of inaccurate elements in derived files, e.g., proof obligations.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • ISCContextRoot
  • ISCMachineRoot
  • ISCEvent
  • IPOSequent

    Returns whether this element is accurate or not.

  • Method Details

    • isAccurate

      boolean isAccurate() throws RodinDBException
      Returns whether this element is accurate or not.
      Returns:
      the accuracy of this element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • setAccuracy

      void setAccuracy(boolean accurate, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the accuracy of this element.
      Parameters:
      accurate - the accuracy of the element: true means it is accurate, false means it is not
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database
